data.title: Reppo Foundation Secures $20M Capital Commitment to Solve Training Data Bottleneck Using Prediction Markets
data.description:
Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands, April 23rd, 2026, Chainwire

The Reppo Foundation has secured $20M in committed strategic capital from Bolts Capital to support Reppo's next phase of protocol development and ecosystem expansion. The commitment prioritizes holders protecting the long-term interests of the Reppo community.

Reppo Labs Co-founder, RG, said - “Prediction markets TAM is expected to reach $1 trillion annual trading volume by the end of the decade. Our core thesis is that prediction Markets will grow beyond betting on sports and world events. We are already seeing Information markets, Opinion markets, and more. We are thrilled to pioneer AI Training Data markets using crypto primitives. Most AI labs are spending a lot on sourcing domain experts as human data continues to be extremely valuable for AI training, providing on-demand feedback to agents and robots in the real world. Why not let domain experts find your data? That's one of the many challenges we are solving using prediction markets for AI training. Over the last month, we have seen over $2M USD in trading volume, validating our thesis. Users are now leveraging the datasets they have sourced to fine-tune Polymarket agents, detect security vulnerabilities, and much more."
"My vision is that Reppo becomes the de facto venue for Human-AI collaboration where AI agents and robots can spin up datanets, pay humans to source our opinions, beliefs, and preferences autonomously, backed by economic stake, while getting access to fresh data every 48 hours. This also reduces dependency on centralized data vendors, APIs, and public data sources."

This capital commitment represents a significant milestone for the Reppo ecosystem and signals growing institutional recognition of how new mechanisms can address AI Training bottlenecks with a stake-backed evaluation layer for the next generation of artificial intelligence systems.

About Reppo

Reppo is building a prediction-market-powered decentralized network where human judgment becomes a verifiable, incentive-aligned signal for AI systems. The protocol operates through a network of specialized Datanets, each created-owned as a prediction market and signal engine — generating trustworthy, preference-rich data that directly supports AI model training, evaluation, fine-tuning, and alignment research.

Each Datanet is purpose-built to process distinct data modalities including text, images, audio, and video, enabling Reppo to serve the increasingly multimodal demands of frontier AI development. By transforming crowd-sourced human judgment into structured, on-chain verifiable signals, Reppo addresses one of the most critical bottlenecks in modern AI: the reliable sourcing of high-integrity training and evaluation data.

Institutional Support and Investor Ecosystem

Reppo is backed by a distinguished group of investors and advisors spanning the AI and Web3 sectors, including Protocol Labs, CVVC, CMS Holdings, and well known angels.

About Bolts Capital

Bolts Capital is an investment firm led by experts in crypto, venture, and public markets committed to backing foundational infrastructure across artificial intelligence and decentralized networks.

Contact

RG Rmadya

rg@reppo.xyz

data.title: United Rentals Stock Leads the S&P 500 Today. Why It's Surging 20%. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Shares of United Rentals soared on Thursday, leading the S&P 500, after it handily topped first-quarter earnings estimates and raised its full-year guidance amid strong growth in nonresidential construction and the mining sector.

After the closing bell on Wednesday, the company reported first-quarter earnings of $9.71 a share, compared with $8.86 a share a year ago and well above Wall Street's expectation of $8.95 a share. First-quarter revenue for the heavy machinery and equipment rental company grew 7% to $3.98 billion, topping the analyst consensus view of $3.87 billion, according to FactSet.

United Rentals stock surged 20% to $960.18 on Thursday, making it the top S&P 500 performer for the session. Shares are on pace for their highest close since Jan. 22, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

The stock is up 32% in April, part of a 19% advance this year.

CEO Matthew Flannery said Thursday on the first-quarter conference call that United Rentals saw growth across all lines of business.

"Markets saw strong growth led by nonresidential construction and infrastructure," Flannery said. "On the industrial side, power and mining and minerals were notable standouts."

Flannery added that United Rentals benefited from a number of new construction projects in the first quarter — spanning healthcare, infrastructure, power, industrial, manufacturing, and data centers.

Rental revenue increased 8.7% to a first quarter record of $3.419 billion, while the average original equipment at cost increased 5.7%. Total fleet productivity increased 2.3%. Meanwhile, used equipment sales decreased 7.2%.

On the strength of its first-quarter performance, United Rentals raised its full-year revenue outlook. The company projects total revenue between $16.9 billion and $17.4 billion, with the midpoint comfortably above Wall Street's $17.07 billion view and up from its previous forecast of $16.8 billion to $17.3 billion.

United Rentals also narrowed its adjusted Ebitda view to between $7.625 billion and $7.875 billion, compared with its previous $7.575 billion to $7.825 billion forecast. The equipment rental company kept its free cash flow forecast steady, expecting between $2.15 billion and $2.45 billion.

"The year is playing out better than we expected just a few months ago. Feedback from the field continues to be optimistic, particularly for large projects," Flannery told analysts Thursday.

Flannery added that United Rentals "expects to be a key partner" for the FIFA World Cup starting in the second quarter.

Construction for the 2026 FIFA World Cup across the U.S., Mexico, and Canada is focused on converting stadiums into FIFA-compliant soccer fields with major renovations already under way, along with infrastructure upgrades in host cities.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: US farm agency to move research and food safety staff from Washington
data.description:
By Leah Douglas

The U.S. Department of Agriculture said on Thursday it will relocate many research and food safety staff from the Washington region as part of its broader reorganization effort.

The agency is in the process of moving most of its Washington-area staff - ​about 2,600 people - to five regional hubs in ​an effort to bring the workforce closer to farmers. Most USDA employees already live outside Washington.

The USDA will move some Washington-based staff of its Economic Research Service and National Institute for Food and Agriculture to their Kansas City offices, it said in a press release. The two agencies were relocated from Washington to Kansas City under the first administration of President Donald Trump.

Some employees of the National Agricultural Statistics Service will also move, the release said.

"This move puts our research institutions outside of the beltway and closer to the land grant universities with talent pipelines who will lead the research and solve the problems facing the future of American agriculture," said Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins in a statement.

The agency will also begin decommissioning its flagship research site in Beltsville, Maryland, the Beltsville Agricultural Research Center, and relocating research programs, according to the release.

BARC staff have complained about unsafe working conditions, though employees also joined lawmakers and farm groups in criticizing USDA's plan to close the center, arguing the relocation process would interrupt research.

The USDA will also move about 200 employees of its Food Safety and Inspection Service to a new National Food Safety Center in Urbandale, Iowa, the agency said.

data.title: W.Africa Crude-Angolan offers sharply weaken despite ongoing Iran supply crisis
data.description:
West African crude differentials have weakened further, traders said on Thursday, with one Angolan grade offered sharply lower via the Platts system, even as Middle East output remains disrupted by the Iran war.

  • Angolan grade Nemba was offered at dated Brent plus $3.15 a barrel via the Platts system, a trader said, down from dated plus $7 earlier in the day. The cargo is for loading on May 26-27.

  • "All weaker," a trader said in reference to West African grades in general.

  • Most West African crude trading takes place in term contracts or bilateral deals and it is relatively rare for companies to trade cargoes via the Platts system, known as the window.

  • Angola plans to load 35 crude oil cargoes in June, up from 34 cargoes scheduled to load in May. Nigeria's loading programmes for June were expected to emerge in the next few days, another trader said.

  • China will have to return to buying large volumes of oil within three weeks after being a big seller during the peak of the Iran supply crisis, the head of trading firm Mercuria said this week. China is normally a big buyer of West African crude.

data.title: Why Is SLAI Stock Tumbling Today?
data.description:
Key points:
  • As per the deal, the price adjustment mechanism was lifted from 110% to 170% of the company's net asset value (NAV) per ordinary share.
  • The company said the final purchase price is subject to a price cap and will in no event exceed $3.20 per American Depositary Share (ADS).
  • In March, Chaince Digital Holdings offered the first proposal to the company.

Shares of SOLAI (SLAI) slumped more than 12% on Thursday after the company announced it had received a revised take-private proposal from Chaince Digital Holdings Inc. to acquire the company for a purchase price of $3.162 per American Depositary Share (ADS).

The company said that the price adjustment mechanism was lifted from 110% to 170% of the company's net asset value (NAV) per ordinary share.

As per the terms of the deal, the final purchase price is subject to a price cap and will not, in any event, exceed $3.20 per ADS.

The Previous Offer

In March, Chaince Digital Holdings made the first proposal to take the company private at $3.069 per ADS, in cash.

The company said the proposed purchase price represented 110% of its net asset value (NAV) per ordinary share as of Sept. 30, 2025. In this offer too, the final purchase price was subject to a price cap of $3.20 per ADS.

SOLAI is a technology-driven cryptocurrency infrastructure company and has been involved in cryptocurrency mining activities and in setting up other blockchain-related ecosystems.

Previously, the company was known as BIT Mining and traded under the ticker "BTCM," but later changed its name to SOLAI in October 2025. The company was founded in 2021, based in Akron, Ohio.

How did Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment surrounding the stock has remained in ‘bearish’ territory amid ‘high’ message volumes.

Shares of SOLAI have risen over 18% this year.

Also Read: OKLO Stock In Spotlight After Nvidia Deal – Why Does HSBC See A 23% Upside?

data.title: Jaguar Land Rover North America Recalling Vehicles Due to Converter Failures
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Jaguar Land Rover North America is recalling various Land Rover and Jaguar vehicles due to problems with the vehicle converters that can cause them to lose driving power.

The company is recalling seven Land Rover models and two Jaguar models because of issues with DC-DC converter failure, according to a National Highway Traffic Safety Administration report.

The converter can fail due to an internal fault in the boost control microchip, according to the report. That can result in a loss of 12-volt system charging and the complete loss of drive power and exterior lighting.

The remedy for the recall is currently under development, according to the report.

The recalled models include 2021-2024 Land Rover Range Rover Velar and Land Rover Discovery, 2020-2023 Land Rover Range Rover Evoque, 2019-2024 Land Rover Range Rover Sport, 2020-2024 Land Rover Range Rover, 2020 Land Rover Discovery Sport, 2020-2024 Land Rover Defender, 2021-2024 Jaguar F-Pace and 2021-2022 Jaguar E-Pace mild-hybrid electric vehicles.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Cannabis rescheduling fuels optimism across industry
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice is immediately loosening restrictions on some marijuana products and accelerating the process of reclassifying the drug as less dangerous in one of the biggest changes to U.S. drug policy in decades.

The Trump administration on Thursday moved FDA-approved marijuana products and state-regulated medical marijuana from a group of drugs classified as highly addictive to one that has a low to moderate potential for abuse.

Here are some comments from company executives and policy experts:

IRWIN SIMON, CEO, TILRAY BRANDS

"Rescheduling has the potential to accelerate clinical research, broaden access and elevate the quality, consistency, and safety standards that establish medical cannabis as a legitimate pillar of modern healthcare."

KIM RIVERS, CEO, TRULIEVE

"This sends a signal that marijuana should not be in the same classification as heroin, and that there is absolutely an acknowledgement, it's a requirement for Schedule 3, that there are acceptable medical uses for marijuana."

INESA PONOMARIOVAITE, FOUNDER AND CEO, NESA'S HEMP

"If this transition is going to deliver real public health benefits, it has to be paired with a commitment to data-driven standards, better education around the endocannabinoid system, and a more collaborative approach across healthcare, regulatory bodies and industry."

ARAS AZADIAN, CEO, AVICANNA

"Since the initial executive order, we have been preparing for what could be a post rescheduling landscape in the U.S. and the opportunities to advance R&D, clinical development and translate our comprehensive platform for cannabinoid-based medicine from Canada into the U.S. market."

MELISSA STURGESS, CEO, ANANDA PHARMA

"For Ananda, it means we can speed up our clinical trials and plan to bring a licensed CBD-based medicine to market for the treatment of endometriosis pain. The millions of women suffering from this debilitating condition deserve a non-hormonal treatment that will relieve their suffering."

BRETT SCHUMAN, CO-CHAIR OF CANNABIS PRACTICE AT GOODWIN

"We can't forget the many people still sitting in federal prison serving life or other lengthy sentences for non-violent cannabis offenses. Today's change in federal law requires that those sentences be revisited and, in most cases, commuted."

MARK LEWIS, PRESIDENT OF SPECIALTY PAYMENTS, LUT

"Dispensary operators still have to figure out how to pay employees, how to accept payments without disruption and how to keep cash flow moving in a system that doesn't fully support your business. That won't change overnight, if at all with rescheduling."

data.title: Snap-on 10-Q: $1,308.3M revenue, $4.69 diluted EPS for quarter
data.description:
Snap-on reported total revenues of $1,308.3 million for the three months ended April 4, 2026, and diluted earnings per share of $4.69, with diluted shares of 52.7 million. The quarter included $101.1 million of Financial Services revenue and produced net earnings attributable to Snap-on of $247.0 million. Gross margin was 50.4%, with operating earnings of $318.8 million that incorporated $68.0 million from Financial Services.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $1,308.3 million (three months ended April 4, 2026), including Financial Services revenue of $101.1 million.
  • Net sales: $1,207.2 million (reported as net sales growth context).
  • Gross profit: $608.3 million; gross margin 50.4% of net sales (impacted by tariffs/material costs; RCI benefits partially offset).
  • Operating earnings: $318.8 million; operating earnings margin 24.4% of revenues (includes $68.0 million from Financial Services).
  • Net earnings: $253.5 million; net earnings attributable to Snap-on was $247.0 million.
  • Diluted net earnings per share: $4.69 per share; diluted shares 52.7 million (three months ended April 4, 2026).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Net sales rose 5.8% year-over-year to $1,207.2 million, driven by a 3.4% organic gain and favorable foreign-currency translation.
  • Channel and product momentum: Broad-based gains across segments, with notable double-digit performance in specialty torque and strength in critical industries.
  • Brand & franchise performance: Snap-on Tools Group showed improved margins and mobile distributor strength, with emphasis on franchisee service and retention.
  • Operational efficiency: RCI (Runway Continuous Improvement) initiatives delivered savings that partially offset tariff and material cost pressures; ongoing manufacturing and supply improvements noted.
  • Outlook & investments: Capital expenditure guidance for fiscal 2026 is approximately $100 million, with continued investment in technology and RCI deployment to support growth and productivity.

Original SEC Filing: Snap-on Inc [ SNA ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: American Express 10-Q: Revenues $18.91B, EPS $4.28
data.description:
American Express reported total revenues net of interest expense of $18,907 million and diluted EPS of $4.28 for the period, reflecting double‑digit top‑line and per‑share growth versus the prior year. Revenue growth was driven by higher billed business and net card fees, while EPS benefited from improved pretax and net income. The company continues to invest in technology and premium member benefits to support acquisition and retention.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues net of interest expense: $18,907 million, up $1,940 million (11%) versus prior year; FX‑adjusted +10%.
  • Gross Profit (Net interest income on statement): $4,692 million, up $523 million (13%).
  • Operating Income (Pretax income): $3,778 million, up $448 million (13%).
  • Net income: $2,971 million, up $387 million (15%).
  • Diluted EPS: $4.28, up $0.64 (18%).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth was underpinned by a 10% increase in billed business and higher net card fees.
  • Channel and spend trends: Goods & services spend rose 10% and travel & entertainment spend increased 12%; retail and airline spending showed momentum despite late‑quarter travel disruptions.
  • Brand and product engagement: Strong engagement with refreshed U.S. Platinum cards and notable adoption among Millennial and Gen‑Z cohorts, supporting acquisition and retention strategies.
  • International and partnerships: International billed business surged 20% (13% FX‑adjusted); the Swisscard acquisition consolidated an international joint venture, expanding global footprint.
  • Operational investments: Continued investments in AI, technology, member services and premium benefits to drive future acquisition, engagement and retention.

Original SEC Filing: AMERICAN EXPRESS CO [ AXP ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: Lockheed Martin Ramps Up Munitions Production Amid War, Spending Skyrockets — Update
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Lockheed Martin is working to rapidly expand munitions production in the face of surging demand, as the U.S. responds to ongoing conflicts and heightened geopolitical tensions.

Those investments in production--coupled with the timing of some billings, long-term debt repayments and cash dividends--caused the defense contractor to burn through more money than expected in the latest quarter. Lower-than-anticipated earnings sent shares down.

The stock fell 4%, to $533.60, on Thursday. Despite the loss, shares are up 7.4% since the beginning of the year.

Lockheed has construction and modernization projects currently in the works at more than 20 of its facilities nationwide, Chief Executive Jim Taiclet said on a call with analysts. The investments will allow the company to increase the scale and speed at which it delivers munitions by improving production infrastructure and capabilities as well as strengthening supply chains.

The deals Lockheed inked with the Pentagon during the recent quarter are supporting the investments, and the company will continue to collaborate with the U.S. government to help meet current weapons needs, Taiclet said.

Lockheed earlier this month secured a $4.7 billion contract from the U.S. government to accelerate production of Patriot interceptors, which have been used extensively by the U.S. and its allies to shoot down Iranian missiles and drones. The contract marks a step toward the Lockheed's goal of more than tripling production of the PAC-3 MSE interceptor by 2030.

Beyond production investments, Lockheed said the timing of some billings, some $1 billion spent on long-term debt repayments and $816 million on cash dividends weighed on cash flow during the latest quarter.

The company reported $291 million of negative free cash flow in the recent first quarter. It still predicts a positive year, calling for free cash flow between $6.5 billion and $6.8 billion in 2026. Lockheed additionally backed its full-year outlook for earnings of $29.35 to $30.25 a share on revenue of $77.5 billion to $80 billion.

For the quarter ended March 29, the company posted a profit of $1.49 billion, compared with $1.71 billion in last year's comparable period. Quarterly earnings of $6.44 a share were below the $6.74 a share that analysts polled by FactSet expected.

Sales ticked up 0.3% to $18.02 billion, missing Wall Street models for $18.22 billion. Growth across both Lockheed's missiles and space businesses was slightly offset by its aeronautics unit, which saw lower volumes. Chief Financial Officer Evan Scott noted that sales were hurt by the shortened fiscal period compared with the prior year.

Separately, Lockheed said Thursday that Peru will buy 12 new F-16 Block 70 aircraft, modernizing its fighter fleet and strengthening its national defense capabilities.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: City Holding reports Q1 2026 net income $31.7M, diluted EPS $2.20; ROA 1.92%
data.description:
City Holding reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $31.7 million and diluted earnings per share of $2.20, with return on assets of 1.92% and return on tangible equity of 19.3%. Net interest income (fully taxable equivalent) for the quarter was $59.89 million and total non-interest income was $19.63 million. The company recorded a $0.6 million provision for credit losses and repurchased 262,017 shares during the quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $31.7 million for the quarter ended March 31, 2026; net income available to common shareholders $31.735 million.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $2.20 for Q1 2026 (basic EPS also $2.20).
  • Net interest income (fully taxable equivalent): $59.89 million for Q1 2026; reported net interest income $59.62 million.
  • Total non-interest income: $19.63 million for Q1 2026.
  • Provision for credit losses: $0.6 million in Q1 2026; allowance for credit losses at period-end $19.713 million.

Business Highlights

  • Asset base: Total assets were $6.760 billion at March 31, 2026, with investment securities representing 21.8% of assets and cash and cash equivalents of $299.0 million.
  • Loan and deposit trends: Gross loans totaled $4.496 billion (down $11.3 million from December 31, 2025); period-end deposits increased $42.6 million to $5.344 billion.
  • Credit quality: Non-performing assets declined to $12.18 million (0.27% of loans and other real estate owned); past due loans were $8.832 million (0.20% of loans).
  • Capital and liquidity: Tangible equity was $637 million (tangible equity ratio 9.65% period-end); City National Bank had approximately $1.8 billion of additional borrowing capacity from Federal Reserve and FHLB facilities and about $762 million of unpledged investment securities.
  • Shareholder actions and operations: Board approved quarterly cash dividend of $0.87 per share and authorized a new share repurchase program of up to 1,000,000 shares; the company repurchased 262,017 shares in Q1 at an average price of $117.79.

Original SEC Filing: CITY HOLDING CO [ CHCO ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: Avis Stock Falls Again; Investors See Possible Equity Offering — Barrons.com
data.description:
Andrew Bary

Avis Budget Group's formerly high-flying stock is down sharply for the second straight day as investors see rising odds of an equity offering that could ease the short squeeze in the shares.

Avis shares have plunged 40%, or $179 a share, to $265 a share, in morning trading Thursday, after declining 38% to $444 on Wednesday. They topped out at a record high of nearly $848 early in the session Wednesday.

Avis said after the close of trading Wednesday that it would release its first-quarter earnings on April 29 before the market open.

The earnings release, coming about a week before the company reported first-quarter earnings in 2025, is weighing on the stock because it could open the door to an equity offering by the rental-car company.

JP Morgan analyst Ryan Brinkman downgraded Avis to Underweight from Neutral in a client note Thursday while raising his price target to $165 from $123 a share.

"We view the recent extraordinary "short-squeeze" driven rally in Avis Budget (CAR) shares as a potentially significant opportunity for management to create lasting value via opportunistic capital market transactions, but are nevertheless downgrading the stock to Underweight from Neutral, given our sense that the shares have risen far above the level we feel can be justified by even the most optimistic view of underlying earnings fundamentals even after according a substantial benefit to our December 2026 price target analysis (which rises +34%, to $165 from $123) to account for capital markets optionality," Brinkman wrote, referring to a potential equity sale by the company.

Avis has 35 million shares outstanding with short interest of nine million shares on March 31, according to Bloomberg data. The company is now valued at about $9 billion.

The apparent spur to the recent squeeze that took the stock up from around $100 on March 20 is that two investors — SRS Investment Management and Pentwater Capital Management — effectively control more than 100% of Avis shares through a combination of ownership and total-return swaps.

The company filed in March to issue up to five million shares through a group of Wall Street sales agents at prevailing market prices.

Such sales could be used to reduce debt. Barron's is unaware of any sales under that authorization, and the view on Wall Street has been that the company is unlikely to sell any of that stock until after its earnings release. Such sales could pressure the stock.

The two large investors in Avis may also be waiting for the earnings release before potentially selling shares.

SRS Investment Management is headed by Jagdeep Pahwa, who is also Avis's executive chairman. SRS, the largest Avis holder with 17.4 million shares plus 2.9 million via swaps, probably can't sell until the company's earnings release given that Pahwa is an insider.

Pentwater held 7.8 million shares, plus 10.2 million via swaps, as of March 25, according to the Avis proxy dated April 2. Florida-based Pentwater is led by Matthew Halbower.

Pentwater is best known for its involvement in takeover arbitrage situations. Pentwater's potential sales of Avis stock may be restricted by the short-swing rule of the Securities Act of 1934 that affect investors with 10%-plus holdings in a stock.

This rule requires 10% holders of a company's stock like Pentwater to return any profits on stock held less than six months to the company. It appears that all but about three million shares of Pentwater's Avis stake have been held for less than six months.

Avis could use proceeds from any equity sale to reduce its $6 billion of corporate debt or for other corporate purposes.

Even after the selloff, Avis is richly valued at about 60 times projected 2026 earnings per share of about $4 a share even after the selloff.

It's also valued at nearly 20 times estimated 2026 Ebitda — earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization — of around $800 million. Historically, Avis has been valued at closer to 10 times Ebitda. Avis has about $6 billion of corporate debt.

Rival Hertz Global Holdings , which has about 75% of Avis's revenues, is valued at $2.1 billion, or about 20% of the Avis market value. Hertz stock is up about 20% this year while Avis shares are still up 100%. Three companies — Avis, Hertz and the private Enterprise — control about 90% of the U.S. rental car market.

Write to Andrew Bary at andrew.bary@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
